data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

Too big if any holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across ~5+ files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, treat Step 0 as a split-first pass.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, decomposed children once more; third-level splits must be rejected with an explanation.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260513T194145-9c09b1df/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing child IDs, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a success. Do not mix implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260513T194145-9c09b1df/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

`.ddx/executions/20260513T194145-9c09b1df/no_changes_rationale.txt` must contain one:

- `verification_command: <cmd>` — repo cwd; exit 0 closes, nonzero rejects.
- `status: open` + `reason: <retryable>` — open, smart retry.
- `status: proposed` + `reason: <operator needed>` — operator lane.
- `status: blocked` + `reason: <external blocker>` — blocked lane.

No pseudo-statuses. Bare rationales and `status: needs_investigation` reject.

To request orchestrator decomposition, add `orchestrator_action: decompose` alongside `status: open`.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260513T194145-9c09b1df/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260513T194145-9c09b1df/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Mode and overrides

DDX_MODE=bead_execution: edit code/docs for bead AC. Only broad queue-steward default is overridden; tracker, merge-policy, verification, safety stay active. Bead description/AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and project defaults.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.
  </instructions>
  <bead id="ddx-bbdbc903">
    <title>readiness: align intake prompt with bead-lifecycle schema</title>
    <description>
PROBLEM
The pre-claim readiness/intake hook is producing malformed `readiness_checks` shapes during live `ddx work` runs, including object-shaped and string-shaped values. Decoder hardening is tracked separately, but there is also a prompt/skill contract mismatch that encourages model drift: the bead-lifecycle skill documents a readiness schema with `readiness_checks` as an array, while the runtime prompt asks for a partially different field set.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `.agents/skills/ddx/bead-lifecycle/SKILL.md:30-31`, `.claude/skills/ddx/bead-lifecycle/SKILL.md:30-31`, and `cli/internal/skills/ddx/bead-lifecycle/SKILL.md:30-31` say `MODE: intake` is compatibility readiness mode unless the caller gives a narrower schema.
- The same skill copies at lines 71-125 document the readiness JSON schema with `tractability`, `score`, `rationale`, `readiness_checks` as an array of objects, `suggested_fixes`, `rewrite`, `suggested_child_beads`, and `waivers_applied`.
- `cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:242-248` builds the live pre-claim prompt with `MODE: intake` but asks for `classification, confidence, reasoning, readiness_checks, suggested_fixes, suggested_child_beads, and waivers_applied`; it omits `tractability`, `score`, and `rationale`, uses legacy `confidence/reasoning`, and also says not to include rewrite fields.
- This split contract leaves the model deciding which schema to follow, so malformed `readiness_checks` values can appear even when the skill itself shows an array example.

PROPOSED FIX
Align the live pre-claim prompt and all shipped bead-lifecycle skill copies around one explicit intake/readiness contract. Either make the runtime prompt quote the exact readiness-mode JSON shape DDx decodes, or make the skill define a deliberately narrower intake schema that exactly matches the runtime prompt. In either case, explicitly state that `readiness_checks` MUST be a JSON array, may be empty, and every entry MUST be an object with `reason`, `verdict`, `evidence`, and `checkable_before_attempt`. Keep the operator-required threshold strict: prompt-quality nits go in `suggested_fixes`; only true blockers become `operator_required`.

NON-SCOPE
Do not replace decoder hardening in ddx-c81c3cad; DDx must still tolerate malformed model output. Do not change readiness classifications or queue state-machine semantics. Do not make readiness failures block work by default.
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. `TestBuildPreClaimIntakePrompt_UsesDocumentedReadinessSchema` or equivalent asserts the generated prompt contains the same field names and shape as the bead-lifecycle readiness schema, including `tractability`, `score`, `rationale`, and `readiness_checks` as an array.
2. `TestBuildPreClaimIntakePrompt_ForbidsScalarReadinessChecks` or equivalent asserts the generated prompt explicitly says `readiness_checks` MUST be a JSON array and must not be an object/string.
3. `.agents/skills/ddx/bead-lifecycle/SKILL.md`, `.claude/skills/ddx/bead-lifecycle/SKILL.md`, and `cli/internal/skills/ddx/bead-lifecycle/SKILL.md` remain synchronized for the readiness/intake JSON contract.
4. `ddx skills check .agents/skills/ddx/bead-lifecycle .claude/skills/ddx/bead-lifecycle cli/internal/skills/ddx/bead-lifecycle` passes, or the repo-equivalent skill validation command passes.
5. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -run "TestBuildPreClaimIntakePrompt_.*|TestPreClaimReadiness_.*|TestReadiness.*" -count=1` passes.
6. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
    </acceptance>
    <labels>area:agent, area:skills, area:bead-lifecycle, kind:bug, phase:build, reliability</labels>
    <metadata spec-id="FEAT-010" base-rev="d765587e610fd00d0ab8b7746df8906b24816056" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260513T194145-9c09b1df"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <ref id="FEAT-010" path="docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md">Feature: Task Execution</ref>
  </governing>
</execute-bead>

data.output:
Aligned the intake prompt to the canonical readiness schema and kept the shipped bead-lifecycle skill copies in sync.

- Prompt builder now asks for `classification`, `tractability`, `score`, `rationale`, `readiness_checks`, `suggested_fixes`, `rewrite`, `suggested_child_beads`, and `waivers_applied`, and explicitly requires `readiness_checks` to be a JSON array, not an object or string: [cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:286](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-bbdbc903-20260513T194145-9c09b1df/cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go#L286)
- Added prompt assertions covering the documented readiness schema and the scalar-`readiness_checks` prohibition: [cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_rewrite_test.go:492](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-bbdbc903-20260513T194145-9c09b1df/cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_rewrite_test.go#L492), [cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_rewrite_test.go:517](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-bbdbc903-20260513T194145-9c09b1df/cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_rewrite_test.go#L517)
- Extended the skill contract test to cover all three shipped copies, including `cli/internal/skills/ddx/bead-lifecycle/SKILL.md`: [cli/internal/agent/bead_lifecycle_skill_contract_test.go:23](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-bbdbc903-20260513T194145-9c09b1df/cli/internal/agent/bead_lifecycle_skill_contract_test.go#L23)
- Updated the shared skill text in all three locations to state that `readiness_checks` must be a JSON array and must not be an object or string: [ .agents/skills/ddx/bead-lifecycle/SKILL.md:164](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-bbdbc903-20260513T194145-9c09b1df/.agents/skills/ddx/bead-lifecycle/SKILL.md#L164), [ .claude/skills/ddx/bead-lifecycle/SKILL.md:164](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-bbdbc903-20260513T194145-9c09b1df/.claude/skills/ddx/bead-lifecycle/SKILL.md#L164), [cli/internal/skills/ddx/bead-lifecycle/SKILL.md:164](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-bbdbc903-20260513T194145-9c09b1df/cli/internal/skills/ddx/bead-lifecycle/SKILL.md#L164)

Verification passed:
- `ddx skills check .agents/skills/ddx/bead-lifecycle .claude/skills/ddx/bead-lifecycle cli/internal/skills/ddx/bead-lifecycle`
- `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -run 'TestBuildPreClaimIntakePrompt_.*|TestPreClaimReadiness_.*|TestReadiness.*' -count=1`
- `lefthook run pre-commit`

Commit: `90cc79177` `fix: align pre-claim intake prompt with readiness schema [ddx-bbdbc903]`
